title: sabulina verna subsp. verna
intro: Sabulina verna subsp. verna, also known as sea lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits of Sabulina verna subsp. verna include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
